mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing, reducing discount rates for equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in unemployment rate above 1.0pp indicates tightening labor markets, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving S&P 500 positive returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain negative when UNRATE declines by >1.0pp over 3mo or Rank IC < 0.02 in labor-driven regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP and housing starts signals strong consumer demand, increasing corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: 60d SPX returns are negative when both GDPC1 and HOUST show positive growth or interaction effect size < 0.15 in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in industrial production above 1.5% signals improving supply chain efficiency, reducing inflationary pressures and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative when INDPRO increases by >1.5% over 2mo or Rank IC < 0.01 in supply-driven regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% over 6 months drives central bank rate hikes, increasing equity discount rates and causing S&P 500 negative returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive despite CPIAUCSL > 2.5% over 6mo or Rank IC > -0.03 in high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in payroll employment and labor force participation indicate weakening labor market conditions, reducing corporate profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20d SPX returns are positive when both PAYEMS and RSAFS decline or interaction effect size > -0.12 in regression models.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month drop in consumer spending (RSAFS) below 400 billion signals weak demand, reducing corporate revenues and causing S&P 500 negative returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive when RSAFS falls below 400B or Rank IC > -0.02 in demand-driven regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate supply-side deterioration, increasing recession risks and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: 60d SPX returns are positive when both INDPRO and HOUST decline or interaction effect size > -0.15 in regression models.
